violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Employee has registered in an approved food safety management course. New Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair, splitting away from the doors. New gaskets have been ordered. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Employee(s) must register in and successfully complete an approved course in food safety management. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

Open employee beverage containers were observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

Ambient temperature of prep unit observed with a temperature of 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. TCS foods were moved to properly functioning units. No TCS foods are to be stored in this unit until it is serviced and maintaining a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

Several foods observed not marked with the date of preparation/slicing. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared/sliced in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation/slicing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

ROP lobster meat observed thawing in its' original package at a temperature of 44 degrees F. ROP foods/seafood must be removed from packaging when thawing to introduce oxygen and prevent foodborne illness due to ROP. Lobster meat was voluntarily disposed of during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

Container of flour observed not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024
Rubber gaskets of 2-door reach-in #1 observed in poor repair, splitting away from the door. Gasket must be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2025
